detailed_analysis: src/agents_sync/tool_specs/claude.py is a thin Layer-3 (Interface Adapter / port-wiring) factory: build_claude_spec(config) closes over a set of small pure adapter functions imported lazily from claude_io / slash_command_io / mcp_server_io, then assembles one frozen AgenticToolSpec describing the five Claude customization_type cells (agent, skill, slash_command, rules, mcp_server). The module is 125 lines, well under the 300-line limit. There is exactly one function of substance, build_claude_spec, which is 108 lines (lines 17-124). It exceeds the project's 40-line function guideline, but its body is almost entirely declarative: five trivial nested closures that forward arguments verbatim, plus one large dataclass literal. There is no branching (cyclomatic complexity 1), no nesting beyond the closure definitions, no mutable state, no I/O, no secrets, no injection surface, and no destructive write path. The lazy intra-function imports (lines 18-28) are a deliberate cycle-avoidance / startup-determinism pattern consistent with the rest of tool_specs and agentic_tool_spec.default_agentic_tools; they are not import side effects. The nested closures (parse_agent, parse_skill, parse_slash_command, render, render_slash_command) are near-identical to those in sibling factories and to the *_factory helpers, hinting at mild duplication, but each tool's wiring is genuinely distinct in its arguments and the project's Rule-of-Three has been partially honoured already by extracting _rules_factory and _mcp_server_factory. Security and correctness axes are clean. The only real structural smell is the single long declarative factory function and a handful of missing parameter annotations / unused parameters in the local closures (an interface-shape concession to the ParseFn Protocol). Spec conformance is strong: the module is exactly the one-module-per-tool shape US-10 AC-1 mandates, references the tool name 'claude' only in adapter/config-key context (Goal 5 / §1), passes config through to the mcp_server cell so secret_policy is evaluated at the egress boundary (Goal 6 / NFR-15), and adds no tool-specific branching to the sync engine (AC-2).

findings.evidence:
def build_claude_spec(config: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None) -> AgenticToolSpec:
    ... 108 lines: five nested closures (lines 30-74) + one AgenticToolSpec literal (lines 76-124) ...
findings.problem: Function spans 108 lines, exceeding the project's 40-line guideline (AGENTS.md §3). Mitigating: cyclomatic complexity is 1 (no branches), the body is purely declarative wiring, and the shape matches every sibling factory in tool_specs/. The length is driven by the five forwarding closures plus a large dataclass literal, not by tangled logic. Downgraded to MINOR because it is structurally a configuration table, not behavioural code.

parse_agent and parse_skill accept artifact_path and artifact_root keyword parameters that are never used in their bodies (lines 30-46). They exist only to satisfy the ParseFn Protocol signature in agentic_tool_spec.py. This is a deliberate interface-shape concession rather than a true defect, but the unused parameters are dead surface a reader must reconcile against the actual call.

findings.variant: Near-duplicate closure boilerplate across tool_specs factories
findings.evidence:
def parse_agent(text, prior_canonical, *, artifact_path=None, artifact_root=None): return parse_claude_md(text, prior_canonical=prior_canonical, kind="agent")
# vs parse_skill: identical but kind="skill"
findings.problem: parse_agent and parse_skill differ only in the literal kind argument; the parse/render closures here closely mirror those in gemini_cli.py and the _rules_factory/_mcp_server_factory helpers. The repetition is contained and the project has already extracted the rules and mcp_server cells into shared factories (Rule of Three partially applied), so this is an observation, not a required refactor. Flagged for awareness only.

findings.evidence:
def render(canonical: dict[str, Any], prior_text: str | None) -> str:
    return render_claude_md(canonical, prior_text=prior_text)
findings.problem: The local closure is named render (line 48), a single bare verb, while its sibling is render_slash_command (line 66). AGENTS.md §4 requires a two-word minimum (subject+mechanism). render_agent or render_claude_md_doc would disambiguate which customization_type it serves. Local scope limits blast radius, hence SMELL.

verdict_summary: Clean thin port-wiring factory; one over-length but branch-free declarative function and minor naming/duplication smells. No security, correctness, or spec deviations.
